meta.title: Maria Sverdlik - Art Teacher
meta.description: Portfolio of an art teacher: teaching philosophy, CV, personal works, student works and contacts.

hero.title: Maria Sverdlik
hero.lead:
Interdisciplinary artist and educator working across drawing, sculpture, jewelry, 3D, and contemporary visual practice.
Welcome to my teaching portfolio.

about.heading: A quiet studio where technique serves freedom.
about.text: I teach drawing, painting and composition to children, teenagers and adults. In class we connect observation, craft and the student's personal interests so the result is not a lucky pretty picture, but a clear step forward.
about.portraitCaption: Replace the photo in the images/site folder.

philosophy.heading: The one who lights the beacons
philosophy.text:
Not all students in my classroom intend to become professional artists. That is perfectly natural. My goal is to develop their visual literacy and critical thinking, skills that will serve them no matter what path they choose.
My background spans literature, cultural studies, 3D sculpting, jewelry design with hot enamel, and visual storytelling. My work also includes academic research, interdisciplinary projects, and humanities writing. This experience helps me guide senior students not only in creating art, but also in structuring their ideas, reflecting deeply, and articulating their creative processes with clarity.
I do not divide art into “fine” and “applied,” digital and analog. Instead, I integrate these disciplines into a structured framework. Art has its own grammar: composition, color, form, rhythm, and symbol. I teach students to master this grammar so they can decode the visual world around them, from museum collections and cinema to advertising and contemporary media.
Art trains essential skills: observing like a scientist, calculating spatial relationships like a mathematician, and decoding meaning like a linguist. In my classroom, I do not showcase my personal style. I teach students to observe deeply, to analyze form, materials, and connections. Through structured, inquiry-based projects, they learn to embrace experimentation, become comfortable with imperfect results, and think through imagery.
This approach is especially powerful for senior students in the IB Diploma Programme, where artistic practice is inseparable from research, reflection, and conceptual development.
The art room is not a space for relentless competition or empty praise. It is a place to slow down, cultivate emotional intelligence, and treat every student’s thoughts with genuine respect. Students are incredibly sensitive to insincerity. They need honesty, structure, and the assurance that their ideas truly matter.
In a world saturated with images and visual noise, an art teacher acts as a thoughtful navigator. My role is modest but essential: to light the beacons. Sometimes, a single well-timed conversation, a technical skill, a relevant research method, or an unexpected cultural connection is enough to help a student take initiative and move forward independently.
That is why art is worth teaching.

cv.intro:
Interdisciplinary educator and creator working at the intersection of classical fine art, 3D technologies, and high-end craftsmanship. I bridge the gap between traditional artistic foundations and contemporary digital practices, training students not just in technical skills, but in conceptual visual thinking and independent portfolio development.

**AREAS OF EXPERTISE**

**FINE ARTS & BASE**
Drawing, Painting, Composition, Perspective, Anatomy, Illustration, Graphic Arts
**3D & DIGITAL PRODUCTION**
3D Sculpting, CG Art, Digital Production, Commercial Video Production
**CRAFT & MATERIAL**
Jewelry Design, Cloisonne Enamel, Mixed Media, Sculpture, Pottering
**MENTORSHIP**
Portfolio Development, Art-direction, Interdisciplinary programs for teenagers

timeline.title: Independent Visual Arts Educator
timeline.text:
Curated Interdisciplinary Programs: Designed and executed original educational tracks for teenagers, blending traditional fine art with contemporary creative media.
Specialized Workshops: Conducted intensive masterclasses in visual thinking, advanced perspective, composition, and authentic cloisonne enamel techniques.
Mentorship: Guided students through portfolio development for international creative — admissions

timeline.title: Freelance CG Artist & CG Producer
timeline.text:
Digital Sculpting & Assets: Developed high-fidelity 3D sculpts and assets for commercial, gaming, and artistic projects.
Creative Production: Managed the production pipeline for commercial videos, international marketing campaigns, and branding concepts.
Packaging Illustration: Created bespoke illustrations and concept art for premium product packaging

timeline.title: Founder & Studio Artist - Private Art Studio
timeline.text:
Business & Art Direction: Launched and managed an independent, self-sustaining art studio. 
Collection Launch: Designed, manufactured, and marketed author's jewelry collections and mixed-media sculptures.
Community Building: Hosted regular studio workshops, creating a hub for local artists and art enthusiasts.
